design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect resources → construct base → upgrade military → conquer opponents through strategic unit composition and map control
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Three asymmetrically balanced races (Protoss, Terran, Zerg) competing for resource dominance with distinct unit abilities and playstyles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command an alien species' military forces across interstellar conflicts; experience three distinct strategic perspectives through campaign narratives
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects rushing meta from original StarCraft through unit cost/ability adjustments; emphasizes strategic depth and tactical positioning over early-game aggression; presents morally complex narrative across three playable factions without clear heroes

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Increased difficulty in single-player campaign with non-linear mission design; greater strategic demand required; AI opponents use tactics more effectively than original game; player must adapt to diverse scenario types

progression.upgrade_system: Unit upgrade advancements available to each race; new units unlock specific tactical options (e.g., Dark Templar merging into spellcaster units)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progression through three episodic campaigns (Protoss, UED Terran, Zerg), each with increasing strategic complexity; new units introduced via mission briefings and campaign advancement
replayability_hooks: Three distinct campaign narratives with different factions and perspectives
New unit additions create strategic variation in mission approaches
Non-linear mission design allows multiple tactical solutions
AI opponents use varied tactics encouraging replay experimentation

world_building_techniques: Cinematic cutscenes at conclusion of each campaign
Mission briefings contextualizing objectives within larger conflict
In-mission conversations between named characters and player commander
Instruction manual exposition establishing political/military context
Character-driven narrative across three playable factions (Zeratul, Aldaris, Jim Raynor, Gerard DuGalle, Sarah Kerrigan)
Continuation of original StarCraft plot: post-Overmind chaos, UED invasion, Zerg fragmentation

faction_indicators: Protoss: Dissident Dark Templar faction vs. mainstream leadership (Aldaris/Zeratul reconciliation)
Terran: UED imperialist force vs. Dominion rebels (Raynor, Duran)
Zerg: Cerebrate swarm hierarchy seeking post-Overmind reorganization under Kerrigan
danger_signals: Rampaging uncontrolled Zerg on Aiur after Overmind's death
UED expeditionary force dispatched to prevent alien contact with Earth
Dominion threat to colonial independence and rebel movements
environmental_storytelling: Protoss homeworld Aiur ravaged by mindless Zerg infestation
Koprulu Sector as contested colonial frontier with three-way power struggle
Racial distinctions reflected in unit design and base construction (psionic Protoss, adaptable Terrans, insectoid Zerg)
